stages.title: Ask
stages.detail:
What happens: You type a question and choose Claude or GPT-4 in the UI. Optional voice input uses the mic button.

The app sends your question to the server with your provider choice.

alternatives.label: Streamlit UI
alternatives.detail: Early prototype UI — replaced by the current React app.

stages.title: Plan
stages.detail:
The system runs a LangGraph planner that may add one or more short search terms (comparison questions are a common case).

All planned queries are merged into one live wiki lookup — not separate fetches per subtask, and not revised from retrieval results.

stages.title: Retrieve
stages.detail:
Live wiki (default):

  • Fused search queries (mechanic terms + topic terms, full question last)
  • Direct page-title probes + overlap ranking of hits
  • Fetch relevant pages, split into passages in memory
  • Cross-encoder rerank → top passages for the answer model

Results are cached between asks. An optional second lookup may run if the first pass looks weak.

alternatives.label: Local 18-page index
alternatives.detail: Small offline index for development — not used on the public demo.

alternatives.label: Hybrid local + live
alternatives.detail: Try a local index first; fall back to live wiki when scores are weak.

alternatives.label: No rerank
alternatives.detail: Skip cross-encoder second pass — not used in production.

stages.title: Generate
stages.detail: Claude or GPT-4 writes using only reranked wiki excerpts (PoE 1, excerpts-only rules).

alternatives.label: Bedrock
alternatives.detail: AWS Bedrock — server-side configuration only; not in the UI provider dropdown.

stages.title: Score
stages.detail:
Default: judges do not run automatically. Click Score response after Ask to run five quality checks.

Judges read the same wiki excerpts as the answer model. Scores do not change the answer or retrieval (post-hoc only).

alternatives.label: Inline on every Ask
alternatives.detail: Run all five judges automatically after each answer (slower; used in some dev setups).

stages.title: Respond
stages.detail:
Always: answer markdown and wiki source links.

Also: pipeline timing bars, Score button, quality metrics, and optional trace panels.
